texts.orig: 17 Appendix D – PX HF MEASUREMENT REPAIR
texts.text: 17 Appendix D – PX HF MEASUREMENT REPAIR

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The emergency PX*1*234 patch addresses PCE V Health Factors Measurements Repair and introduces the option PX HF MEASUREMENT REPAIR. This option is used to edit/delete corrupted data measurements in V Health Factor entries due to a bug in patch PX*1*211.
texts.text: The emergency PX*1*234 patch addresses PCE V Health Factors Measurements Repair and introduces the option PX HF MEASUREMENT REPAIR. This option is used to edit/delete corrupted data measurements in V Health Factor entries due to a bug in patch PX*1*211.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patch PX*1*211 had a bug, when a health factor (HF) that had a measurement defined was given to a patient, the VHF entry had the UCUM CODE stored even though a measurement was not being recorded. Patch PX*1*217 corrected the bug.
texts.text: Patch PX*1*211 had a bug, when a health factor (HF) that had a measurement defined was given to a patient, the VHF entry had the UCUM CODE stored even though a measurement was not being recorded. Patch PX*1*217 corrected the bug.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When the PX*1*234 patch is installed, an automatic repair of the corrupted data is applied. Members of the PCE Management Mail Group will receive one and possibly two MailMan messages. Each of these messages will have a different course of action.
texts.text: When the PX*1*234 patch is installed, an automatic repair of the corrupted data is applied. Members of the PCE Management Mail Group will receive one and possibly two MailMan messages. Each of these messages will have a different course of action.

texts.text: 17.3.1 HEALTH FACTORS WITH INCOMPLETE MESUREMENT DEFINITION

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When a message with the subject: HEALTH FACTORS WITH INCOMPLETE MEASUREMENT DEFINITION is received, it means that one or more health factors having an incomplete measurement definition were found during the installation process. The user can add the missing fields using the Health Factor Management menu option.
texts.text: When a message with the subject: HEALTH FACTORS WITH INCOMPLETE MEASUREMENT DEFINITION is received, it means that one or more health factors having an incomplete measurement definition were found during the installation process. texts.label: text
texts.orig: The editor displays the allowed range and units for the measurement. In the above example, a human can interpret “EIGHTY” in the COMMENTS field to be the number 80 and recognize that it is in the inclusive range 5 to 300, so the MAGNITUDE can be set to 80.
texts.text: The editor displays the allowed range and units for the measurement. In the above example, a human can interpret “EIGHTY” in the COMMENTS field to be the number 80 and recognize that it is in the inclusive range 5 to 300, so the MAGNITUDE can be set to 80.

texts.orig: As you work through the list, there will be COMMENTS where it is straight forward to determine a magnitude and others where a magnitude cannot be determined. When a magnitude cannot be determined, the UCUM CODE should be deleted.

texts.orig: When there is a valid measurement, both MAGNITUDE and UCUM CODE must be stored in the VHF entry. If there is no measurement, then neither MAGNITUDE nor UCUM CODE should be present. If only one of those two fields is present it can cause Health Summary errors.
texts.text: When there is a valid measurement, both MAGNITUDE and UCUM CODE must be stored in the VHF entry. If there is no measurement, then neither MAGNITUDE nor UCUM CODE should be present. If only one of those two fields is present it can cause Health Summary errors.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Shown below are the two LCS health factors measurement definitions and some actual COMMENTS from a site. Each comment is followed by a suggestion for what to do about setting the magnitude. In the suggestions, CNBD stands for Cannot be Determined, in those cases if the UCUM CODE is present it should be deleted.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In determining packs/day, it is helpful to know a pack contains 20 cigarettes.
texts.text: In determining packs/day, it is helpful to know a pack contains 20 cigarettes.

texts.label: text
texts.orig:
COMMENTS:
3/4   =  0.75
4 CIGARS PER DAY = CNBD, this is for cigarette smoking only.
"3 days to finish pack" = 0.33
"I never smoked a pack in a day" = CNBD 
chews all day. = CNBD, this is for smoking cigarettes, not chewing.
unkn  = CNBD
3- 4 cans per week  = CNBD, this is for smoking cigarettes, not chewing.
1 CIGARETTE DAILY  = 1/20 = 0.05
3 cig/day = CNBD, this is for cigarette smoking only.

COMMENTS:
3 cigarettes a day for 60 years = 60
10 cigarettes per day = CNBD
>15 years = 15
pt reports smoking 1 pack daily for over 40 yrs = 40
unsure = CNBD

40+ years = 40

table_cells.text:
This is the encounter date/ time for primary encounters or the date for occasions of service. If the encounter is related to an appointment, this is the appointment date/time. If this is an occasion of service created by an ancillary package, this is the date/time of the instance of care.  
Imprecise dates are allowed for historical encounters.  
Encounter date/time may be added, but not edited. *Deletions of encounters can occur only when nothing is pointing to the encounter
This is the patient DFN. This cannot be edited or deleted
This is the hospital location where the encounter took place for primary encounters, or this is the ordering location for ancillary encounters. Not required if the Service Category is “E”
This encounter is related to a service connected condition
This encounter is related to Combat Veteran
This encounter is related to Agent Orange exposure
This encounter is related to Ionizing Radiation exposure
This encounter is related to SW Asia Conditions
This encounter is related to Project 112/SHAD
This encounter is related to Military Sexual Trauma
This encounter is related to Head and/or Neck Cancer via Nose and/or Throat Radium treatment
This is the date/time when the encounter was checked out
This is the eligibility of the patient for this encounter
This denotes the type of encounter

A::=Ambulatory
Should be used for clinic encounters.
“A” s are changed to “I”s by Visit Tracking if patient is an inpatient at the time of the encounter.
H::=Hospitalization
Should be used for an admission.
I::=In Hospital
C::=Chart Review
T::=Telecommunications
N::=Not Found
S::=Day Surgery
O::=Observation
E::=Event
(Historical) Documents encounters that occur outside of this facility. Not used for workload credit or 3rd party billing. 
R::=Nursing Home
D::=Daily Hospitalization Data
X::=Ancillary Package Daily Data
“X” s are changed to “D”s by Visit Tracking if patient is an inpatient at the time of the encounter

*This is required for ancillary occasions of service such as laboratory and radiology or telephone encounters. If Hospital Location is specified, this will be set automatically, so in most cases it is not needed
This is the appointment type of the encounter. It is not stored in the Visit file but is included on the “ELAP” node of the data published by PXK VISIT DATA EVENT
Free text location of service if outside the VA.  If set, then the type of visit is set to “Other”
Facility of service. If set, then the type of visit is set to “VA”

Pointer to the Location file (#9999999.06)   This field points to the Institution file (#4) and has the same internal number as that file. The Location has the same name as the Institution file (#4). The location is also referred to as the Facility

This identifies the type of encounter, e.g., primary encounter, ancillary encounter, etc.  
A “Primary” designation indicates that the encounter is associated with an appointment or is a standalone. 
Examples of ancillary encounters include Laboratory and Radiology instances of care

Set of Codes.
P::=Primary
O::=Occasion of Service
S::=Stop Code
A::=Ancillary  
Ancillary packages such a Laboratory and Radiology 
should pass an “A”
C::=Credit Stop
This is the parent encounter for which the ENCOUNTER is a supporting encounter.  For example, this would be the primary encounter for which this occasion of service supports and should be associated
This is the PFSS Account reference
This is a flag that denotes deletion of the encounter entry.  Encounter will not be deleted if other data is pointing to it

table_cells.text: Indicator that denotes this provider as the “primary” provider for the encounter
Indicator that denotes this provider as the attending provider

A pointer to the Package file #(9.4), or the name of the package, or the Prefix. This only needs to be included if it is different than the Package stored with the Visit. Note that this field is uneditable, once it has been set it cannot be changed

A pointer to the PCE Data Source file (#839.7) or a string of text (3-64 characters) identifying the source of the data. The text is the SOURCE NAME field (.01) of the PCE Data Source file (#839.7). If the SOURCE currently does not exist in the file, it will be added. This only needs to be included if it is different than the Data Source stored with the Visit. Note that this field is uneditable, once it has been set it cannot be changed
This is a flag that denotes deletion of the Provider entry

table_cells.text: Code or Pointer to ICD Diagnosis file (80)
Code that specifies that the diagnosis is the “primary” diagnosis for this encounter.  Only one “primary” diagnosis is recorded for each encounter
Code that specifies that the diagnosis is either an “ordering” diagnosis or is a “resulting” diagnosis or both for this encounter
This is a term that is contained in the Clinical Lexicon
This is the problem IEN that is being acted upon.  *This node is required to edit an existing problem on the Problem List
*This is required to Add a diagnosis/problem to the Problem List. “1” indicates that the entry should be added to the Problem List
This documents whether a problem is active or inactive. The Default is Active if not specified
The date that the problem began
The date that the problem was resolved
The provider’s description of the diagnosis/problem.  *If NARRATIVE is not passed for a diagnosis/problem, the Description from the ICD Diagnosis file (80) will be used as the default
A term that denotes a grouping or category for a set of related diagnosis/problem
If the ICD code documents a procedure, this is the provider who ordered it.

DX Free Text  (1-245 char)
PL  Free Text (3-60 char)

A pointer to the Package file #(9.4), or the name of the package, or the Prefix. This only needs to be included if it is different than the Package stored with the Visit. Note that this field is uneditable. Once it has been set, it cannot be changed

A pointer to the PCE Data Source file (#839.7) or a string of text (3-64 characters) identifying the source of the data. The text is the SOURCE NAME field (.01) of the PCE Data Source file (#839.7). If the SOURCE currently does not exist in the file, it will be added. This only needs to be included if it is different than the Data Source stored with the Visit. Note that this field is uneditable. Once it has been set, it cannot be changed
This is a delete flag used to denote deletion of the diagnosis entry

table_cells.text: Series specifies the sequence of the series for the immunization that was administered

0 ::=None
1 ::=Fever
2 ::=Irritability
3 ::=Local reaction or swelling
4 ::=Vomiting
5 ::=Rash or itching
6 ::=Lethargy
7 ::=Convulsions
8 ::=Arthritis or arthralgias
9 ::=Anaphylaxis or collapse
10 ::=Respiratory distress
11 ::=Other
This field may be used to indicate that this immunization should not be administered again. “1” indicates that the immunization should not be given to the patient in the future
This is the reason for overriding the warning of existing contraindication and/or refusal  reasons
This field indicates if this immunization was ordered by policy.
The lot number of the Immunization entered for this event
The source of the information obtained for this immunization event
Pointer to Immunization Info Source file (#920.1)
The method this vaccine was administered
Pointer to Imm Administration Route file (#920.2)
The area of the patient's body through which the vaccine was administered
Pointer to Imm Administration Site (Body) file (#920.3)
The amount of vaccine product administered for this immunization
The units that reflect the actual quantity of the vaccine product administered
Pointer to the UCUM Codes file (#757.5)
The Vaccine Information Statement (VIS) offered to or given to the patient before administration of the immunization, and the date it was offered or given

Format: VISIEN^DATE
"VISIEN" is a pointer to the Vaccine Information Statement file (#920). "DATE" is a date (without time) in FileManager internal format.
Note:  If the caller is updating a previously recorded immunization: 
1) If the caller passes in VIS data in the "VIS" subscript, the system will purge the previously filed VIS data before filing the updates.
2) If the caller does not pass in any VIS data, the previously filed VIS data persists.
3) If the caller wants to delete the previously filed VIS without replacing it with anything else, that is done explicitly by setting the "VIS" subscript as follows: "IMMUNIZATION",i,"VIS")="@"
Comments related to the immunization encounter with the patient

Free-text in the format of a FileManager word-processing field.
Note:  If the caller is updating a previously recorded immunization:
1) If the caller passes in remarks in the "REMARKS" subscript, the system will purge the previously filed remarks before filing the updates.
2) If the caller does not pass in any remarks, the previously filed remarks persist.
3) If the caller wants to delete the previously filed remarks without replacing it with anything else, that is done explicitly by setting the "REMARKS" subscript as follows: "IMMUNIZATION",i, "REMARKS")="@"
The provider who ordered the immunization.
This field indicates acknowledgement of a contraindication/refusal event warning for this immunization with the decision to proceed with administration.
This is the reason for overriding the warning of existing contraindication and/or refusal reasons.
This is the interpretation of the inoculation result.
This is the objective, measurable reading following the inoculation.
This is the date and time of the reading of the immunization results.
This is the name of the person who read and interpreted the results of the inoculation.
This is a comment related to the reading of the patient's inoculation.
